title: thelocactus conothelos subsp. aurantiacus
intro: Thelocactus conothelos subsp. aurantiacus, also known as Thelocactus conothelos 'Aurantiacus', is a rare and lesser-known species of cactus, part of the Thelocactus genus, characterized by its vibrant orange flowers and short, stout stems. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds and reducing pain. Consuming Thelocactus conothelos subsp. aurantiacus has been linked to several health benefits, such as reducing the risk of certain cancers and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
